title: The King and The Queen - Echoes of Utopia
description: A high-performance revival of the iconic The King and The Queen bosses from Orespawn. Optimized for 1.12.2 with a ground-up engine rewrite and legacy 1.7.10 authenticity.
body:
> **Modpack Permission:** Modpack creators have full permission to use this mod in public packs and in videos as long as credit is provided.

# Echoes of Utopia
**A High-Performance Boss Revival for Minecraft 1.12.2**

Echoes of Utopia brings the legendary encounters of the classic 1.7.10 era into 1.12.2 with a complete, ground-up engine rewrite. This mod resurrects the iconic encounters of Orespawn's The King and The Queen, transforming them from notoriously lag-heavy entities into highly stabilized, endgame challenges. No longer can these bosses be easily cheesed by OP weapons. 

---

## Technical Optimization
Echoes of Utopia was engineered to be as lightweight as possible without sacrificing the apocalyptic scale of the fights. 

* **Zero-Lag Explosions:** Custom explosion logic completely eliminates the thousands of item drops that traditionally freeze servers during heavy bombardments. 

* **Optimized AI Radar:** The massive block targeting scans are specifically throttled to run exactly twice a second, reducing server CPU load by over 80%.

* **Client-Side Visuals:** All heavy particle mathematics and rendering loops are strictly isolated to the client's GPU (@SideOnly). 

---

## Boss Mechanics
The King and The Queen are designed to sit at the absolute top of the 1.12.2 modding food chain, just as they once did. They feature original code to read, reject, and punish "god-tier" modded weapons.

* **Strict Damage Caps:** The Royals feature a hard-coded damage cap. Weapons that typically one-shot bosses (even these two in the original mod) will no longer work.

* **Scalable Difficulty:** The system dynamically adjusts to your party. More players increase the bosses' regeneration factor, while the bosses' damage output increases as their own health drops.

* **The "Flickering" Bug Fixed:** I am sure all of us recall how The King and Queen vanished merely a few blocks away depending on your angle of view. No more! By increasing their tracking render range, they now remain solid entities that can be seen from hundreds of blocks away. *(Note: Occasional culling may still happen if you are standing directly inside them and looking too high up due to the sheer size of their models. Should this happen, merely adjust your view angle to a lower angle).*

### The Encounters
* **The Queen:** The chaotic, terraforming matriarch who alternates between a peaceful, flower-planting wanderer and the enraged, laser-firing monster of destruction we all remember. She features a new sinister red fog when enraged to match her tone, alongside a permanent dynamic boss bar that is activated when enraged.

* **The King:** The true monstrosity of two phases. The Ultimate King may be awoken by those who dare feed The Adult Prince the new Queen Scale block. The King now features a permanent boss bar to track his massive health pool.
---

## Dimensions & Gear
Beyond the bosses, Echoes of Utopia brings back the tools and dimensions needed to face them, fully manageable via the **kingandqueen.cfg** file for those who need to alter dimension or biome IDs.

* **The Brown Ant Utopia Dimension:** The original 1.7.10 Utopia dimension was notoriously laggy, a freak of generation that even high-end PCs couldn't walk in. It has been completely reworked and tested on a quad-core 1.3GHz CPU to ensure minimal lag. The terrain is now desolate and clean, serving as an actual stable arena for the massive structures and classic large trees without melting your computer.

* **The 6-Level Challenge Towers:** Rising from the Unstable Ant Dimension are the legendary challenge towers. Whereas in the Golden Age you had to brave all six levels of grueling combat, the dimension is now desolate, but the structures remain. The Royal Guardian Set, the Queen Set, and the Big Bertha await you at the bottom floor, exactly where they have always been.

* **Classic Weapon Mechanics:** Big Bertha and the Royal Guardian Sword return with their iconic visual flair, appearing as 2D sprites in your inventory but rendering as massive 3D models in your hand, seamlessly bypassing the removal of 1.7's `IItemRenderer`. To give you a fighting chance against the Royals, the 1.9+ combat cooldown has been completely disabled for these legendary weapons. Spam click to your heart's content!

* **Relics of the Past:** To survive these two, you need the old-school tools. The classic Notch Apple crafting recipe has been revived, alongside the new Titanium Apple. Additionally, the new Soul Rune serves as a localized keep-inventory mechanic without needing server commands. Crafted with the new Spirit Crystal (found only in the Outer End Islands), it will spare your items upon death as long as it remains anywhere in your inventory.

---

## FAQ

> **Q: Is this an official OreSpawn port?**
> A: No. This is a standalone, optimized "Boss Gauntlet" project using archival assets from the 1.7.10 mod.

> **Q: Does this include the full OreSpawn mod?**
> A: No. This mod focuses specifically on the King and Queen boss experiences. 

---

## Legacy & Credits
The original OreSpawn mod is an undeniable pillar of Minecraft modding history, and a mod that nobody could ever forget. However, its legacy was unfortunately bogged down by years of community drama, controversy, and its final abandonment following Richard Clark's departure from the internet in 2024.

**To the Community:** It is time to heal and simply enjoy these two titans again. This project strips away the baggage, the toxicity, and the bloated mechanics of the past, distilling the experience down to what actually mattered: fighting impossibly cool and impossibly hard bosses!

I urge you to view this as a **Transformative Preservation Project**, however. The entire codebase of this mod, including the AI, combat logic, targeting systems, new feature systems such as the Scalable Difficulty, the new Queen fog, and rendering math, is 100% original and written entirely from scratch due to the vast architectural differences between the 1.7.10 and 1.12.2 environments. All legacy visual, model, and audio assets utilized in this specific release are included strictly to preserve these historically significant, now-abandoned encounters in a version that has aged remarkably well and continues to host the largest hardcore modding community. 

I claim no ownership over the original OreSpawn property (owned by Richard Clark / TheyCallMeDanger). This is a free, passion-driven tribute designed to rescue these iconic bosses from being lost to time and deliver them exactly as they were, only now in a much more stable environment.
